skill.name: amazon-product-api-skill
skill.description: Extracts structured Amazon product listings including titles, ASINs, prices, ratings, and specifications for market research and price monitoring.
skill.summary: Automate Amazon product searches and extract structured data using the BrowserAct API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 63 patterns including network calls, environment variable access, and markdown backtick formatting misinterpreted as shell commands. After evaluation, all findings are false positives: network calls target only the legitimate BrowserAct API service (api.browseract.com), env var access reads only BROWSERACT_API_KEY using standard os.getenv(), and backtick detections are markdown code formatting in SKILL.md, not shell execution. The CRITICAL heuristic was tripped by the standard combination of network + credential + command patterns expected in any API integration skill. No malicious intent, obfuscation, data exfiltration, or unauthorized endpoints found.

low_findings.title: API Key Read from Environment Variable
low_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY via os.getenv() to authenticate with the BrowserAct API. This is standard API key management and the key is only sent to the documented BrowserAct API endpoint. The env var declaration in SKILL.md metadata is for configuration discovery, not extraction.

low_findings.title: Network Calls to External API Service
low_findings.description: The script makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com for task creation, status polling, and result retrieval. All calls target the documented BrowserAct service that the skill is built to integrate with.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API Template ID and Base URL
low_findings.description: TEMPLATE_ID and API_BASE_URL are hardcoded as configuration constants for the BrowserAct service. These are service identifiers, not secrets.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Analyzer Heuristic False Positive: Code Execution + Network + Credential Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged a heuristic combination of network calls, credential access, and command execution as potentially dangerous. Upon review, this is the standard architecture of an API integration skill: a Python script that reads an API key from environment variables, sends it as a Bearer token to a documented API endpoint, and is invoked via shell command. No obfuscation, data exfiltration, or unauthorized endpoints detected.

content.user_title: Search Amazon Products and Extract Structured Listings
content.value_statement: Manually collecting Amazon product data is slow and inconsistent. This skill automates structured data extraction from Amazon search results, returning titles, prices, ratings, and specifications in a clean format.
actual_capabilities: Search Amazon products by keywords with brand and language filters
Extract structured product data including ASINs, prices, ratings, and URLs
Paginate through multiple pages of search results automatically
Handle international Amazon marketplaces by specifying language codes
Monitor long-running search tasks with real-time status logging
Return product specifications such as color, material, and style attributes
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key to authenticate and run searches
Cannot bypass Amazon rate limiting or anti-bot detection measures
Search scope is limited to publicly listed Amazon product pages
Execution time increases with the number of pages requested

use_cases.title: Market Research and Brand Analysis
use_cases.description: Search product categories to analyze top brands, pricing trends, and market positioning across multiple pages of results.

use_cases.title: Competitor Price Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track competitor product listings, current prices, and discounts to inform pricing strategy and identify market shifts.

use_cases.title: Product Database Building
use_cases.description: Extract structured product data with ASINs, specifications, and ratings to build or enrich a product information database.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for wireless earbuds on Amazon and show me the top results with prices and ratings.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to find products by keyword and see basic listing information.

prompt_templates.title: Brand-Filtered Search with Pagination
prompt_templates.prompt: Find Samsung phones on Amazon across 3 pages of results and list the top sellers.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs to filter by brand and expand the search scope beyond the first page.

prompt_templates.title: International Marketplace Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the German Amazon marketplace for kitchen appliances from Bosch and show me the results.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to research products in a specific regional marketplace using language codes.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Brand Competitive Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for laptops from Dell, HP, and Lenovo. For each brand, extract prices, ratings, and best seller badges across 5 pages.
prompt_templates.scenario: A power user needs to run multiple searches to build a competitive landscape report.

output_examples.input: Search for wireless earbuds on Amazon
output_examples.output: Found 20 products for 'wireless earbuds'. Top results include Sony WF-1000XM5 at $298.00 (4.5 stars, 12,000 ratings), Apple AirPods Pro at $249.00 (4.7 stars, 50,000 ratings, Best Seller badge), and Bose QuietComfort Earbuds at $279.00 (4.3 stars, 8,500 ratings).

output_examples.input: Search German Amazon for Bosch kitchen appliances
output_examples.output: Searching Amazon in German (de). Found 15 Bosch kitchen appliances. Top results include Bosch Serie 6 Dishwasher at 849.00 EUR (4.6 stars), Bosch MUM5 Stand Mixer at 199.99 EUR (4.4 stars, Amazon's Choice badge), and Bosch TDS6080 Steam Iron at 129.99 EUR (4.3 stars).

best_practices: Set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before running the skill to avoid authentication failures.
Start with one page of results to verify the search criteria, then increase pages as needed.
Monitor the terminal output during execution to track task status and detect any issues early.
anti_patterns: Retrying more than once when a task fails without an authorization error wastes API quota and time.
Running the skill for real-time price monitoring at high frequency may exceed rate limits.
Ignoring the API key requirement and expecting the skill to return results without configuration.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a BrowserAct API key. Visit the BrowserAct Console at browseract.com to create an account and get your key.

faq.question: How long does a product search take to complete?
faq.answer: Most searches complete within a few minutes. The time depends on the number of pages requested and current API response speed.

faq.question: Can I search Amazon in different languages or regions?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify a language code such as 'de' for German or 'zh-CN' for Chinese to search localized Amazon marketplaces.

faq.question: What product data does the skill extract from Amazon?
faq.answer: It extracts product titles, ASINs, URLs, brand names, current and original prices, average ratings, rating counts, and featured badges.

faq.question: What happens if my API key is invalid or expired?
faq.answer: The skill detects invalid authorization errors and stops without retrying. It guides you to verify and update your API key.

faq.question: Can I filter search results by brand and control how many pages to scan?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify a brand name to filter products and set the number of search result pages to paginate through.
